intro: Melicope christophersenii, also known as Christophersen's melicope, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Bioactive constituents of Melicope christophersenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and limonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds.
